data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Amazon uses a microservices architecture to enable agility and scalability.
data.technical_spec: Multiple loosely coupled services communicating typically over HTTP/JSON.

data.title: Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Amazon AWS provides backbone for hosting and compute needs.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes services like EC2, S3, Lambda, and more for operational efficiency.

data.title: APIs and Gateway Layers
data.description: APIs serve as a communication layer between various microservices.
data.technical_spec: API Gateway supports routing and load balancing among services.

data.title: Global Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: CDN reduces latency and improves load times for global users.
data.technical_spec: Uses Amazon CloudFront to cache content near user locations.

data.title: Data Lake and Warehousing
data.description: Amazon operates a large-scale data lake architecture for data analytics.
data.technical_spec: Integrates AWS Athena, Redshift, and Glue for big data processing.
